_meta.description: Default merchant disposition presets for Dynamic Bartering. 'personality' affects acceptance thresholds, counter-offer behavior, and relationship volatility. 'category' marks the merchant's specialty so they haggle more readily on goods they actually deal in (and resist off-specialty items).

personalities.fair: Balanced merchant. Standard acceptance thresholds, moderate patience.
personalities.generous: Kind-hearted. More willing to accept lower offers, slower to anger.
personalities.greedy: Profit-driven. Less likely to accept deals in the player's favour, quick to counter.
personalities.sleazy: Shady dealer. Doesn't mind lowball offers but will try to fleece you. High counter rate.
personalities.stern: No-nonsense. Offended by lowballs, rarely counters (take it or leave it).
personalities.timid: Nervous or meek. Easily intimidated, accepts most reasonable offers quickly.

categories.generalgoods: General trader; deals in most everyday goods.
categories.magic: Court wizard / magic trader: spell tomes, staves, soul gems, enchanted gear.
categories.fence: Thieves Guild fence; deals in stolen goods.
categories.caravan: Khajiit travelling trader; buys almost anything.

Belethor.note: General goods merchant in Whiterun, known for his shady demeanor and willingness to buy/sell anything.

Adrianne Avenicci.note: Blacksmith in Whiterun. Hardworking and professional.

Eorlund Gray-Mane.note: Skyforge smith. Proud Nord craftsman, doesn't suffer fools.

Arcadia.note: Alchemist in Whiterun. Friendly and helpful.

Hulda.note: Innkeeper at the Bannered Mare. Businesslike.

Fralia Gray-Mane.note: Jewelry vendor in Whiterun. Kind elderly woman.

Carlotta Valentia.note: Produce/food vendor in Whiterun. Hardworking single mother.

Ysolda.note: Aspiring merchant in Whiterun. Eager and friendly.

Elrindir.note: Runs The Drunken Huntsman in Whiterun; sells weapons and hunting supplies.

Lucan Valerius.note: General goods in Riverwood. Friendly shopkeeper.

Alvor.note: Blacksmith in Riverwood. Helpful and welcoming.

Orgnar.note: Innkeeper at Sleeping Giant Inn. Laconic.

Delphine.note: Secret Blade operating inn in Riverwood.

Farengar Secret-Fire.note: Court wizard of Whiterun. Considers himself above merchants.

Enthir.note: Black market dealer at the College of Winterhold. Shady Bosmer; magic and soul gems.

Birna.note: Struggling shopkeeper in Winterhold. Desperate for business.

Niranye.note: Fence in Windhelm. Shady Altmer merchant.

Revyn Sadri.note: General goods in Windhelm. Honest Dunmer trying to make a living.

Oengul War-Anvil.note: Blacksmith in Windhelm. Proud Nord craftsman.

Nurelion.note: Alchemist in Windhelm. Obsessed with his research, grudging about sales.

Angeline Morrard.note: Alchemist in Solitude. Kind old woman worried about her daughter.

Evette San.note: Spiced wine vendor in Solitude. Cheerful.

Beirand.note: Blacksmith at Castle Dour in Solitude.

Gulum-Ei.note: Fence in Solitude. Thieves Guild contact.

Endarie.note: Radiant Raiment clothing store in Solitude. Haughty Altmer.

Taarie.note: Radiant Raiment clothing store in Solitude. Snobbish Altmer.

Sybille Stentor.note: Court wizard of Solitude. Cold and calculating.

Grelka.note: Armor & leather vendor in Riften. Surly and unpleasant.

Bersi Honey-Hand.note: Pawned Prawn owner in Riften. Easily pressured.

Balimund.note: Blacksmith in Riften. Proud of his craft.

Keerava.note: Innkeeper at Bee and Barb. Tough Argonian.

Talen-Jei.note: Works at Bee and Barb. Friendly Argonian.

Calcelmo.note: Court wizard of Markarth. Obsessed scholar.

Bothela.note: Alchemist in Markarth. Old Breton woman.

Frabbi.note: Works at Silver-Blood Inn. Constantly arguing about money.

Muiri.note: Alchemist's assistant in Markarth. Shy and hurt.

Frida.note: Alchemist in Dawnstar. Kind elderly woman.

Thoring.note: Innkeeper at Windpeak Inn in Dawnstar.

Lami.note: Alchemist in Morthal. Sweet and caring.

Jonna.note: Innkeeper at Moorside Inn in Morthal.

Valga Vinicia.note: Innkeeper at Dead Man's Drink in Falkreath.

Zaria.note: Alchemist in Falkreath. Deals in poisons, sinister undertone.

Babette.note: Dark Brotherhood vendor. 300-year-old vampire child; alchemy and poisons.

Ri'saad.note: Khajiit caravan leader. Charming but shrewd.

Dremora Merchant.note: Conjured Daedric merchant. Only cares about the deal.

Milore Ienth.note: Alchemist in Raven Rock. Friendly Dunmer.

Glover Mallory.note: Blacksmith in Raven Rock. Thieves Guild ties.

Neloth.note: Telvanni wizard on Solstheim. Supremely arrogant.
